key_insight: A confined-space CVD approach with controlled precursor placement and thermal ramps enables deterministic growth of high-quality Mo(W)Te2 crystals, alloys, and atomically sharp lateral/vertical heterostructures.
engineering_relevance: This provides a scalable route to reproducible Te-based heterointerfaces, supporting device-ready quantum and topological material platforms with improved structural control.
method: Confined-space CVD lowers the effective growth temperature window.
Use tailored precursor configurations to steer crystal/alloy/heterostructure formation.
Apply stepwise thermal ramps to promote deterministic interface engineering.
evidence_signal: Aberration-corrected STEM, STM, and STS directly verify nearly atomically sharp Mo(W)Te2 boundaries and distinct local density-of-states distributions.
watch_item: Te-based dichalcogenides remain structurally sensitive and may still require tight control of temperature, precursor geometry, and interface cleanliness for reproducible scale-up.
paper_title: Controlled chemical vapor deposition for synthesis of emerging Mo(W)Te2 systems
